title: Kerbrute User Enumeration for Valid Accounts
description: This runbook operationalizes Kerbrute User Enumeration for Valid Accounts using #kerbrute . It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments. Finds valid domain usernames and pre-auth edge cases before spraying/roasting.
shortDescription: Kerbrute User Enumeration for Valid Accounts

metadata.descriptionHtml:
This runbook operationalizes Kerbrute User Enumeration for Valid Accounts using #kerbrute. It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments.

Finds valid domain usernames and pre-auth edge cases before spraying/roasting.

  1. Prepare scope and access: validate target reachability and select low-noise execution order.
  2. Execute core workflow: run the command chain to produce actionable security outcomes.
  3. Validate and operationalize output: confirm results, capture artifacts, and prepare follow-on actions.
  • Tool Location/Source: primary references are in local source corpus entries linked from runbook-list.md; e.g., docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/0x1ceKing-HTB-Certified-Penetration-Testing-Specialist/cheatsheet/Active Directory Enumeration and Attacks.md:321; docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/0xsyr0-OSCP/README.md:3340.
  • Attack pre-requisite: authorized scope, operator shell access, and protocol reachability on TCP/UDP 88.
  • What attacker gain: validated findings (access paths, credentials, execution context, or exposure evidence) enabling faster compromise progression.
  • Detection/Defense opsec: monitor abnormal auth/enumeration bursts, enforce least privilege and hardening baselines, and alert on repeated tool-pattern telemetry.

Core Commands:

kerbrute userenum -d inlanefreight.local --dc 172.16.5.5 /opt/jsmith.txt
./kerbrute userenum -d <DOMAIN> --dc <DOMAIN> /PATH/TO/FILE/<USERNAMES>
kerbrute userenum --dc IP -d DOMAIN users.txt
metadata.commandsBlock:
kerbrute userenum -d inlanefreight.local --dc 172.16.5.5 /opt/jsmith.txt
./kerbrute userenum -d <DOMAIN> --dc <DOMAIN> /PATH/TO/FILE/<USERNAMES>
kerbrute userenum --dc IP -d DOMAIN users.txt
